title: BAA Compliance Evidence — Control Attestations vs Technical Telemetry

compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.52' running on sourcetype hipaa:baa_attestation and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:baa_attestation" earliest=-365d
| where answer IN ("Yes","FullyImplemented")
| join vendor_id, control_id [
    search index=metrics sourcetype="hipaa:ba_control_metric" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(measured_value) as measured by vendor_id, control_id ]
| eval gap=if(isnull(measured) OR measured<95,"Evidence_Gap","OK")
| where gap!="OK"
| table vendor_id, control_id, answer, measured, gap
description: Joins annual BA questionnaire answers (encryption, logging) with Splunk-ingested technical proof points so subcontractor compliance is evidence-based, not checkbox-only.
value: Joins annual BA questionnaire answers (encryption, logging) with Splunk-ingested technical proof points so subcontractor compliance is evidence-based, not checkbox-only.
implementation: (1) Map each attestation row to an automated metric (for example TLS coverage percent from vulnerability scans); (2) legal sets materiality thresholds; (3) feed gaps into vendor risk reviews.
visualization: Matrix (vendor x control), Table (gaps only), Column chart (percent evidence coverage).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Joins annual BA questionnaire answers (encryption, logging) with our monitoring platform-ingested technical proof points so subcontractor compliance is evidence-based, not checkbox-only — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:baa_attestation` (`vendor_id`, `control_id`, `answer`), `index=metrics` `sourcetype=hipaa:ba_control_metric` (`vendor_id`, `control_id`, `measured_value`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map each attestation row to an automated metric (for example TLS coverage percent from vulnerability scans); (2) legal sets materiality thresholds; (3) feed gaps into vendor risk reviews.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:baa_attestation" earliest=-365d
| where answer IN ("Yes","FullyImplemented")
| join vendor_id, control_id [
    search index=metrics sourcetype="hipaa:ba_control_metric" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(measured_value) as measured by vendor_id, control_id ]
| eval gap=if(isnull(measured) OR measured<95,"Evidence_Gap","OK")
| where gap!="OK"
| table vendor_id, control_id, answer, measured, gap
```

Understanding this SPL

**BAA Compliance Evidence — Control Attestations vs Technical Telemetry (§164.308(b)(3) / §164.502(e))** — Joins annual BA questionnaire answers (encryption, logging) with Splunk-ingested technical proof points so subcontractor compliance is evidence-based, not checkbox-only.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:baa_attestation` (`vendor_id`, `control_id`, `answer`), `index=metrics` `sourcetype=hipaa:ba_control_metric` (`vendor_id`, `control_id`, `measured_value`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: hipaa:baa_attestation. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="hipaa:baa_attestation", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where answer IN ("Yes","FullyImplemented")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap!="OK"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **BAA Compliance Evidence — Control Attestations vs Technical Telemetry (§164.308(b)(3) / §164.502(e))**): table vendor_id, control_id, answer, measured, gap


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix (vendor x control), Table (gaps only), Column chart (percent evidence coverage).
